title: xerochloa littoralis
intro: Xerochloa littoralis, also known as coastal chaff or coastal grass, is a species of grass native to coastal regions of Australia and Asia, characterized by its dense tufts of stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. Consuming Xerochloa littoralis has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Xerochloa littoralis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
